name: Alikhan
description: Alikhan is a compound of two separate elements rather than a single root: Ali, from the Arabic ʕaliyy, 'elevated, exalted' (root ʕ-l-w, 'to be high'), and khan, the Turkic and Mongolic title for a ruler or chieftain. The combination — a revered Islamic name plus a title of rule — is common across the Caucasus and Central Asia, including wide use as a Kazakh given name.
